question.content: Using the y=100 baseline and the positions corresponding to ages 15, 30, and 85 in the original figure, write the values of the requested Q(a). Answer format: Q(15),Q(30),Q(85) in that order, separated by commas; write only the final results, with no units, explanatory text, or equals signs.

step_1.content: Mark the baseline y=100 and locate ages 15, 30, and 85 on the original chart so each Q(a) can be read as a vertical ratio.
step_1.key_point: Numerator is the male-female vertical gap and denominator is the higher curve offset from 100.

step_3.content: At age 15, the separation between two curves is large while the higher curve is still only moderately above 100, so the ratio is the largest.
step_3.key_point: Q(15) is maximal among the three target ages.

step_5.content: At age 30, the curves are nearly coincident near 100 so the numerator is tiny; at age 85 the denominator magnitude is very small while a visible gap remains.
step_5.key_point: Therefore Q(30) is smallest and Q(85) stays between Q(15) and Q(30).
